metadata.summary: A blog application for Django sites, inspired by classic WordPress.

documentation:
# DJ Press

A blog application for Django sites, inspired by classic WordPress.

> Warning - very alpha.

## Versioning

This package uses semantic versioning, but until we reach version 1.x.x, the following rules will apply:

- MAJOR version will remain on 0 until the base functionality is complete.
- MINOR version indicates that an incompatible or breaking change has been introduced.
- PATCH version indicates a bug fix or a backward compatible change.

If you choose to use this package prior to version 1.x being release, please pin your requirements to a specific minor version, e.g. `djpress~=0.11.0`

## Installation

- Install `djpress` by adding it to your requirements file, e.g. `djpress~=0.11.0` (see versioning note, above).
- Add it to your `INSTALLED_APPS` in Django:

```python
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    "djpress.apps.DjpressConfig",
    # ...
]
```

- Add the URLs to your project's main `urls.py` file.

The following will set DJ Press to be available at the root of your site, e.g. `https://example.com/`

```python
from django.urls import path, include

urlpatterns = [
    # ...
    path("", include("djpress.urls")),
    # ...
]
```

If you want your blog to be in a subdirectory, use something like the following:

```python
from django.urls import path, include

urlpatterns = [
    # ...
    path("blog/", include("djpress.urls")),
    # ...
]
```

- Run migrations: `python3 manage.py migrate`

> Note that this relies on the Django Admin for content management, so ensure that you have a user with at least `staff` status to manage content.

## Configuration

In your settings.py file, create a `DJPRESS_SETTINGS` dictionary. Here are some common settings:

```python
# DJPress settings
DJPRESS_SETTINGS = {
    "BLOG_TITLE": "My Awesome Blog",
    "POST_PREFIX": "{{ year }}/{{ month }}",
}
```

There are lots more settings available. Please checks the docs or look at the source code:
[src/djpress/app_settings.py](https://github.com/stuartmaxwell/djpress/blob/main/src/djpress/app_settings.py)

## Documentation

Documentation is a work-in-progress but is available on GitHub Pages: <https://stuartmaxwell.github.io/djpress>

## Badges

[![codecov](https://codecov.io/github/stuartmaxwell/djpress/graph/badge.svg?token=IOS7BCD54B)](https://codecov.io/github/stuartmaxwell/djpress)

Returns:

modules.name: djpress.models.post
documentation: Post model.

classes.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager.get_queryset
documentation: Return the queryset for pages.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager.get_published_pages
documentation:
Return all published pages.

For a page to be considered published, it must meet the following requirements:
- The status must be "published".
- The date must be less than or equal to the current date/time.
- All parent pages must also be published.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager.get_published_page_by_slug
documentation:
Return a single published page.

Args:
    slug (str): The slug of the page.

Returns:
    Post: The published page.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager.get_published_page_by_path
documentation:
Return a single published page from a path.

The path can consist of one or more pages, e.g. "about", "about/contact". We split the path into parts and
check the page is valid. These are the checks to be made:

1. The first part of the path must be a top-level page.
2. Subsequent parts must be children of the previous page.
3. The final page must be published.

Args:
    path (str): The path to the page.

Returns:
    Post: The published page.

Raises:
    PageNotFoundError: If the page cannot be found.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PagesManager.get_page_tree

documentation: Page custom manager.

Returns all published posts.

For a post to be considered published, it must meet the following requirements:

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager.get_recent_published_posts
documentation:
Return recent published posts.

This does not return a paginated queryset. Use get_paginated_published_posts
instead.

If CACHE_RECENT_PUBLISHED_POSTS is set to True, we return the cached queryset.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager._get_cached_recent_published_posts
documentation:
Return the cached recent published posts queryset.

If there are any future posts, we calculate the seconds until that post, then we
set the timeout to that number of seconds.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager._get_cache_timeout
documentation:
Return the timeout for the cache.

If there are any future posts, we calculate the seconds until that post.

Args:
    queryset: The queryset of published posts.

Returns:
    int | None: The number of seconds until the next future post, or None if
    there are no future posts.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager.get_published_post_by_slug
documentation:
Return a single published post.

Args:
    slug (str): The post slug.
    year (int | None): The year.
    month (int | None): The month.
    day (int | None): The day.

Returns:
    Post: The published post.

Raises:
    PostNotFoundError: If the post is not found in the database.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager.get_published_posts_by_category
documentation:
Return all published posts for a given category.

Must have a date less than or equal to the current date/time for a specific
category, ordered by date in descending order.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.PostsManager.get_published_posts_by_author
documentation:
Return all published posts for a given author.

Must have a date less than or equal to the current date/time for a specific
author, ordered by date in descending order.
Post custom manager.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.clean
documentation: Custom validation for the Post model.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post._check_circular_reference
documentation:
Check for circular references in the page hierarchy.

This is a recursive function that checks if the current page is an ancestor of itself. This is needed to ensure
that we don't create a circular reference in the page hierarchy. This is called in the clean method.

For example, we need to avoid the following page hierarchy from happening:
- Page A
    - Page B
        - Page C
            - Page A

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    ValidationError: If a circular reference is detected.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.content_markdown
documentation: Return the content as HTML converted from Markdown.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.truncated_content_markdown
documentation: Return the truncated content as HTML converted from Markdown.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.is_truncated
documentation: Return whether the content is truncated.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.url
documentation:
Return the post's URL.

Returns:
    str: The post's URL.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.permalink
documentation:
Return the post's permalink.

The posts permalink is constructed of the following elements:
- The post prefix - this is configured in POST_PREFIX and could be an empty
  string or a custom string.
- The post slug - this is a unique identifier for the post. TODO: should this be
  a database unique constraint, or should we handle it in software instead?

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.full_page_path
documentation:
Return the full page path.

This is the full path to the page, including any parent pages.

Returns:
    str: The full page path.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.is_published
documentation:
Return whether the post or page is published.

For a post to be published, it must meet the following requirements:
- The status must be "published".
- The date must be less than or equal to the current date/time.

For a page to be published, it must meet the following requirements:
- The status must be "published".
- The date must be less than or equal to the current date/time.
- All ancestor pages must also be published.

Returns:
    bool: Whether the post is published.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.is_parent
documentation:
Return whether the post is a parent page.

Returns:
    bool: Whether the post is a parent page.

methods.name: djpress.models.post.Post.is_child
documentation:
Return whether the post is a child page.

Returns:
    bool: Whether the post is a child page.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.categories_html
documentation:
Return the HTML for the categories.

Note this isn't a template tag, but a helper function for the other template tags

Args:
    categories: The categories.
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the categories.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The HTML for the categories.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.category_link
documentation:
Return the category link.

This is not intended to be used as a template tag. It is used by the other
template tags in this module to generate the category links.

Args:
    category: The category.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.get_page_link

Return the page link.

This is not intended to be used as a template tag. It is used by the other
template tags in this module to generate the page links.

Args:

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.post_read_more_link
documentation:
Return the read more link for a post.

Args:

    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.
    read_more_text: The text for the read more link.

Returns:

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.get_blog_pages_list
documentation:
Return the HTML for the blog pages list.

This expects to be passed the output of the get_page_tree method.

Args:
    pages: The pages from the get_page_tree method.
    li_class: The CSS class(es) for the list item.
    a_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.
    ul_child_class: The CSS class(es) for the child ul

Returns:
    str: The HTML for the blog pages list.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.parse_post_wrapper_params
documentation:
Parse the parameters for the template tag.

Args:
    params: The parameters for the template tag.

Returns:

methods.name: djpress.templatetags.helpers.BlogPostWrapper.render
documentation: Render the blog post content.

Wraps the blog post content.

This is a template tag that wraps the blog post content in a configurable HTML tag with a CSS class.

Args:
    nodelist: The content to wrap.
    tag: The HTML tag to wrap the content in.
    css_class: The CSS class(es) for the tag.

Returns:
    str: The wrapped content.

modules.name: djpress
documentation: djpress module.

modules.name: djpress.views
documentation:
DJ Press views file.

There are two type of views - those that return a collection of posts, and then a view
that returns a single post.

functions.name: djpress.views.dispatcher
documentation: Dispatch the request to the appropriate view based on the path.

functions.name: djpress.views.entry
documentation:
The main entry point.

This takes a path and returns the appropriate view.

functions.name: djpress.views.index
documentation:
View for the index page.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    posts (Page): The published posts as a Page object.

functions.name: djpress.views.archive_posts
documentation:
View for the date-based archives pages.

There will always be a year at least.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.
    year (str): The year.
    month (str): The month.
    day (str): The day.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    posts (Page): The published posts for the date as a Page object.

functions.name: djpress.views.category_posts
documentation:
View for posts by category.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.
    slug (str): The category slug.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    posts (Page): The published posts for the category as a Page object.
    category (Category): The category object.

functions.name: djpress.views.author_posts
documentation:
View for posts by author.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.
    author (str): The author username.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    posts (Page): The published posts by the author as a Page object.
    author (User): The author as a User object.

functions.name: djpress.views.single_post
documentation:
View for a single post.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.
    slug (str): The post slug.
    year (str | None): The year.
    month (str | None): The month.
    day (str | None): The day.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    post (Post): The post object.

functions.name: djpress.views.single_page
documentation:
View for a single page.

Args:
    request (HttpRequest): The request object.
    path (str): The page path.

Returns:
    HttpResponse: The response.

Context:
    post (Post): The page object.

Raises:
    Http404: If the page is not found.

This returns the most recent published posts, and tries to be efficient by checking if there's a `posts` object we
can use.
Tags starting with `get_` are used to get data from the database.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.blog_pages_list
documentation:
Returns an HTML list of the blog pages. The pages are sorted by menu order and then by title. Pages that have children have a nested list of children. The default output with no arguments is an unordered list with no classes. CSS classes can be added to the output by specifying the arguments shown below. ```
  • Page 1
  • Page 2
  • Page 3
    • Child 1
    • Child 2
``` Args: ul_outer_class (str): The CSS class(es) for the outer unordered list. li_class (str): The CSS class(es) for the a_class (str): The CSS class(es) for the anchor tags. ul_child_class (str): The CSS class(es) for the nested unordered lists. Returns: str: The HTML list of the blog pages.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.blog_pages
documentation:
Return the pages of the blog.

Args:
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the pages.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The pages of the blog.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.blog_page_title
documentation:
Return the page title.

Args:
    context: The context.
    pre_text: The text to prepend to the page title.
    post_text: The text to append to the page title.

Returns:
    str: The page title.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.have_posts
documentation:
Return the posts in the context.

If there's a `post` in the context, then we return a list with that post.

If there's a `posts` in the context, then we return the posts. The `posts` should
be a Page object.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    list[Post]: The posts in the context.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_post_title
documentation:
Return the title of a post.

This is just the title of the post from the current context with no further HTML.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    str: The title of the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_title
documentation:
Return the title link for a post.

If the post is part of a posts collection, then return the title and a link to the post. If the post is a single
post, then return just the title of the post with no link. But this behavior can be overridden by setting
`force_link` to `True`.

The outer tag can be any of the following: "h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6", "p", "div", "span". If the outer tag
is not one of these, then the title will be returned with no outer tag.

If the outer tag is one of the allowed tags, and if Microformats are enabled, then the outer tag will have the class
"p-name".

Otherwise return an empty string.

Args:
    context: The context.
    outer_tag: The outer HTML tag for the title.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.
    force_link: Whether to force the link to be displayed.

Returns:
    str: The title link for the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_post_author
documentation:
Return the author display name.

Tries to display the first name and last name if available, otherwise falls back to
the username.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    str: The author display name.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_author
documentation:
Return the author link for a post.

Args:
    context: The context.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The author link.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_category_link
documentation:
Return the category links for a post.

TODO: do we need this?

Args:
    category: The category of the post.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_post_date
documentation:
Return the date of a post.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    str: The date of the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_date
documentation:
Return the date link for a post.

Args:
    context: The context.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The date link for the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_content
documentation:
Return the content of a post.

If the post is part of a posts collection, then we return the truncated content of
the post with the read more link.

If the post is a single post, return the full content of the post.

The outer tag can be any one of the following: "section", "div", "article", "p", "span". If the outer tag is not one
of these, then the content will be returned with no outer tag.

If there's an outer tag, and if microformats are enabled, then the outer tag will have the class "e-content".

If there's no post, return an empty string.

Args:
    context: The context.
    outer_tag: The outer HTML tag for the content.
    read_more_link_class: The CSS class(es) for the read more link.
    read_more_text: The text for the read more link.

Returns:
    str: The content of the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.category_title
documentation:
Return the title of a category.

Expects there to be an `category` in the context set to a Category object. If
there's no category in the context or category is not a Category object, then return
an empty string.


Args:
    context: The context.
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the category.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    pre_text: The text to prepend to the category title.
    post_text: The text to append to the category title.

Returns:
    str: The title of the category formatted with the outer tag and class if
    provided.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.author_name
documentation:
Return the name of an author.

Expects there to be an `author` in the context set to a user object. If there's no
author in the context or author is not a User object, then return an empty string.

Args:
    context: The context.
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the author.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    pre_text: The text to prepend to the author name.
    post_text: The text to append to the author name.

Returns:
    str: The name of the author formatted with the outer tag and class if
    provided.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_categories
documentation:
Return the categories of a post.

Each category is a link to the category page.

Args:
    context: The context.
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the categories.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The categories of the post.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.is_paginated
documentation:
Return whether the posts are paginated.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    bool: Whether the posts are paginated.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_pagination_range
documentation:
Return the range of pagination pages.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    range: The pagination pages.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_pagination_current_page
documentation:
Return the current page number.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    int: The current page number.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.pagination_links
documentation:
Return the previous and next post links.

This checks if there is a Page object in the context. If there is, then we return
the previous and next post links. If there is no Page object in the context, then
we return an empty string.

Args:
    context: The context.

Returns:
    str: The previous and next post links.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.page_link
documentation:
Return the link to a page.

Args:
    page_slug: The slug of the page.
    outer: The outer HTML tag for the page link.
    outer_class: The CSS class(es) for the outer tag.
    link_class: The CSS class(es) for the link.

Returns:
    str: The link to the page.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.get_rss_url
documentation:
Return the URL to the RSS feed.

Returns:
    str: The URL to the RSS feed.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.rss_link
documentation:
Return an HTML link to the RSS feed.

If the RSS feed is enabled, then return an HTML link tag that points to the RSS feed. Otherwise, return an empty
string.

Example:
    ```django
    {% rss_link %}
    ```

    ```html
    <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="Latest Posts" href="/rss/">
    ```

Returns:
    str: The HTML link to the RSS feed.

functions.name: djpress.templatetags.djpress_tags.post_wrapper_tag
documentation:
Parse the blog post wrapper tag. This is a template tag that wraps the blog post content in a configurable HTML tag with a CSS class. Example usage: {% post_wrap "article" "post" %}

Post content

{% end_post_wrap %} Args: parser: The template parser. token: The template token. Returns: BlogPostWrapper: The blog post wrapper tag.

modules.name: djpress.url_utils
documentation: URL patterns for the djpress application.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.regex_post
documentation:
Convert the post prefix to a regex pattern.

This will match the following URL parts:
- The year in the format of 4 digits.
- The month in the format of 2 digits.
- The day in the format of 2 digits.
- The slug in the format of word characters or hyphens.

Args:
    prefix (str): The post prefix that is configured in the settings.

Returns:
    str: The regex pattern.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_post_url
documentation: Return the URL for the post.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.regex_archives
documentation:
Generate the regex path for the archives view.

The following regex is used to match the archives path. It is used to match
the following patterns:
- 2024
- 2024/01
- 2024/01/01

Returns:
    str: The regex pattern.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.regex_page
documentation:
Generate the regex path for pages.

The following regex is used to match the path. It is used to match the
any path that contains letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, and slashes.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.regex_category
documentation: Generate the regex path for the category view.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.regex_author
documentation: Generate the regex path for the author view.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_path_regex
documentation: Return the regex for the requested match.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_author_url
documentation: Return the URL for the author's page.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_category_url
documentation: Return the URL for the category.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_archives_url
documentation: Return the URL for the archives page.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_page_url
documentation: Return the URL for the page.

functions.name: djpress.url_utils.get_rss_url
documentation:
Return the URL for the RSS feed.

If the RSS path is not set, the default is "/feed". This will raise an error if the path is not set.

Returns:
    str: The URL for the RSS feed.

modules.name: djpress.plugins
documentation: Plugin system for DJ Press.

classes.name: djpress.plugins.Hooks
documentation: Available hook points in DJ Press.
Hook definitions

classes.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry.__init__
documentation: Initialize the registry.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry.register_hook
documentation:
Register a callback function for a specific hook.

Args:
    hook_name (Hooks | str): The name of the hook - this can be referenced either as the plain text name of
        the hook (e.g. `"pre_render_content"`) or as a Hooks enum member (e.g. `Hooks.PRE_RENDER_CONTENT`).
    callback (callable): The function to call when the hook is triggered.

Raises:
    TypeError: If hook_name is not a Hooks enum member.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry.run_hook
documentation:
Run all registered callbacks for a given hook.

Args:
    hook_name (Hooks): The name of the hook to run, this should be in the form of a Hooks enum member.
    value: The value to be modified by the callbacks.
    *args: Additional positional arguments to pass to the callbacks.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments to pass to the callbacks.

Returns:
    The value after all callbacks have been run.

Raises:
    TypeError: If hook_name is not a Hooks enum member.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry.load_plugins
documentation:
Load all plugins.

Plugins need to be added to the DJPRESS_SETTINGS['PLUGINS'] list.

Plugins can be added to DJPRESS_SETTINGS['PLUGINS'] in two ways:
1. Just the package name (e.g., "djpress_example_plugin") which will look for Plugin class in plugin.py
2. Full path to the plugin class (e.g., "djpress_example_plugin.custom.MyPlugin")

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry._import_plugin_class
documentation:
Import the plugin class from either custom path or standard location.

Args:
    plugin_path: Either full path to plugin class or just the package name.

Returns:
    The plugin class.

Raises:
    ImproperlyConfigured: If plugin cannot be loaded from either location.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.PluginRegistry._instantiate_plugin
documentation:
Create and set up a plugin instance.

Args:
    plugin_class: The plugin class to instantiate.
    plugin_path: The original plugin path (used for error reporting).
    plugin_settings: Dictionary of settings for all plugins.

Returns:
    An initialized plugin instance.

Raises:
    ImproperlyConfigured: If plugin cannot be instantiated or set up.

A registry for plugins.

Handles plugin discovery, loading, and hook management.

classes.name: djpress.plugins.DJPressPlugin

methods.name: djpress.plugins.DJPressPlugin.__init__
documentation: Initialize the plugin.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.DJPressPlugin.setup
documentation:
Set up the plugin.

Args:
    registry (PluginRegistry): The plugin registry.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.DJPressPlugin.get_data
documentation:
Get this plugin's stored data.

Returns:
    dict: The plugin's stored data, or empty dict if none exists.

methods.name: djpress.plugins.DJPressPlugin.save_data
documentation:
Save this plugin's data.

Args:
    data: The data to store for this plugin.
Base class for DJ Press plugins.
